# DLS CSS Library

[![DLS Community Repository](https://img.shields.io/badge/DLS-community-green.svg)](https://github.com/philips-internal/dls)

DLS CSS is a CSS only library implementing the Philips Design Language System
(DLS). It contains styling for most DLS components and simple behavior possible
with pure HTML / CSS. It is also meant to expose the styling in CSS classes to
be inherited by the javascript framework toolkits, which can be used for more
complex projects.

## Show me a demo

Open the [documentation website](https://dls-css.philips-internal.com) for a
demo of the components included.

The library is still missing some components, if you want to contribute check
our [contribution guidelines](CONTRIBUTING.MD).

If you have questions about how to use DLS CSS,
[reach out to us.](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/chat/0/0?users=tijmen.van.gurp@philips.com,henri.schroter@philips.com&topicname=DLS-CSS)

## Installing DLS CSS

For quickstart there's a
[Github Template including React](https://github.com/philips-internal/dls-css-react-template)
that includes DLS CSS and some examples.

Following is the manual process to add DLS CSS to an existing project.

### Using Philips Artifactory (requires VPN)

Artifactory requires the following `.npmrc` file at the root of your project:

```
@dls:registry = https://artifactory-ehv.ta.philips.com/artifactory/api/npm/dl-common-npm
```

A terminal command to create the file:

```bash
echo '@dls:registry = https://artifactory-ehv.ta.philips.com/artifactory/api/npm/dl-common-npm' >> .npmrc
```

To install the package run `npm install @dls/css --save` or `yarn add @dls/css`.

To upgrade to the latest version run `npm install @dls/css@latest --save` or
`yarn add @dls/css@latest`

## Using DLS CSS

After installation add these links to your HTML to start using the library.

This library uses CSS custom properties a.k.a. CSS vars to provide color to the
components. These are kept in separate files to keep the size of the library
small. The available colors can be found in the
[colors folder](https://github.com/philips-internal/dls-css/bundle.zip) in the
bundle. You can nest any number of color themes by applying the classes down the
DOM tree.

Apply the colors for your chosen DLS theme by importing the right color files in
the HTML header:

```html
<!-- Import all components components -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/@dls/css/dls.css" />

<!-- Import content, navigation and accent color variables -->
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="node_modules/@dls/css/colors/vars-group-blue-ultra-light.css"
/>
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="node_modules/@dls/css/colors/vars-group-blue-bright.css"
/>
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="node_modules/@dls/css/vars-accent-orange-ultra-light.css"
/>
<link
  rel="stylesheet"
  href="node_modules/@dls/css/vars-accent-orange-bright.css"
/>
```

Then, make sure to add the corresponding classes for the colors that will be
applied to the element and its children. For example:

```html
<body class="group-blue-ultra-light accent-orange-ultra-light">
  <header class="navigationbar group-blue-bright accent-orange-bright"></header>
  <main></main>
</body>
```

The `group-blue-ultra-light` theme is set on the body setting the overall theme,
while the navigationbar header has the darker `group-blue-bright` colors
applied.

To set the density for components, which mainly the height, you can add the css
class `dense` or `regular` to the parent, the default is `touch` which is the
largest size.

Now you can start using components by copy and pasting them from the
[documentation website](https://dls-css.philips-internal.com).

### Color theme using SASS mixins

Alternatively you can use SASS to import the required themes for your project

```scss
@import '@dls/css/colors/vars-pink-very-light-mixin';
@import '@dls/css/colors/vars-pink-bright-mixin';
@import '@dls/css/colors/vars-accent-orange-ultra-light-mixin';
```

if you just need one theme you only need to set the :root variable

```scss
:root {
  @include pink-very-light();
  @include accent-orange-ultra-light();
}
```

if you need different themes include these under a different scope e.g.
`.dls-navigation-context`

```scss
.dls-navigation-context {
  @include pink-bright();
}
```

### Import individual components

Next to the full library you can also import individual components instead of
the full package:

```html
<!-- Import individual components -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/@dls/css/components/button.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/@dls/css/components/textbox.css" />
```

Or using CSS to limit HTTP requests:

```css
@import 'node_modules/@dls/css/components/button.css';
@import 'node_modules/@dls/css/components/textbox.css';
```

### Use DLS CSS in React project

```js
import '@dls/css/dls.css'
```

The individual components are also available as CSS modules to be scoped locally
in React:

```js
import '@dls/css/components/button.module.css'
```

### Download release via github

Another way to use this library is by downloading the release via
[dls-css release](https://github.com/philips-internal/dls-css/releases) and add
the extracted zip to your project root. Then add the following to your html for
a basic setup:

```html
<head>
  <!-- Import components -->
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="bundle/dls.css" />

  <!-- Import content, navigation and accent colors -->
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="bundle/colors/vars-group-blue-ultra-light.css" />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="bundle/colors/vars-group-blue-bright.css" />
  <link
    rel="stylesheet"
    href="bundle/colors/vars-accent-orange-ultra-light.css"
  />
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="bundle/colors/vars-accent-orange-bright.css" />
</head>

<body class="group-blue-ultra-light accent-orange-ultra-light">
  <header class="navigationbar group-blue-bright accent-orange-bright"></header>
  <main></main>
</body>
```

### I get strange results when combining this with another framework

To prevent class or element conflicts with other frameworks you can
alternatively use:

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="node_modules/@dls/css/prefix-dls.css" />
```

and use the .dls-css class to apply it at that position in the DOM. The new
[cascade layers](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@layer)
standard will make this easier in the future.

## Development

### `npm run start`

To run a watcher on the website build, this is the best view to develop
components if you want to contribute ;)

### `npm run build`

To build the website to the 'dist' folder.

### `npm run bundle`

Builds the bundle in the bundle folder

### `npm run new-component new-component-name`

Adds a new boilerplate component
